title: Extend TabulatedFunction to support 2D and 3D interpolation.

body:
supersedes #5150

Let's see if we can get a preview now.

- https://clima.github.io/OceananigansDocumentation/previews/PR5159/

ok, from my side this is done. Please feel free to adjust/merge/update as needed if this moves forward.

@navidcy @giordano @glwagner @simone-silvestri  @rafferrari 

A short recap:

- [x] now DocumenterVitepress.jl is used to render the documentation.
- [x] In normal Documenter `format = Documenter.HTML(..., canonical=)` was doing the connection to the deploying repo, here this is specified by using the option `deploy_decision = Documenter.deploy_folder(..., repo=)`.
- [x] and now instead of `deploydocs` we use `DocumenterVitepress.deplodocs`, which mirrors the functionality to deploy different versions into different new folders, i.e, `dev`, `stable`, `previews/PR` and `tagged` versions. 
- [x] I grouped different topics into common sections in order to have a more user friendly experience when exploring the site, please see it in action in the preview (link from above). 
- [x] I `created dark/light palettes`, all in blue-ish tones, ocean-like inspiration 💙. Switch between the light and dark mode in the preview.
- [x] I took  🌊 as the input prompt in chatgpt to create a logo. Looks quite good IMO.  Feel free to update or remove 😢.
- [x] updated the landing page using the new logo, name of the package and description from readme. 
- [x] fixed some YouTube videos rendering issues 
   -  There are more (dead links), but those should be fixed in different PRs, since they are really not related to this setup. 
   - [x] math syntax needed an additional plugin, `@mdit/plugin-mathjax`

closes #4891
title: Setup vitepress for the docs

body:
This PR updates the implementation of the generalized vertical discretization (what we now call `MutableVerticalDiscretization`) so that horizontal derivatives are interpreted as horizontal derivatives at constant `z`. I believe this is necessary for code to produce similar / same outcomes for any vertical coordinate. In other words, if we have a static vertical discretization, we assume that horizontal derivatives apply at constant `z`. So when we change vertical coordinates, we need to specify that derivatives still should apply at constant `z`.

The fact that this simplifies the code is demonstrated in how it allows us to remove a term from the tendency. Previously we had a term `grid_slope_contribution_x`... which part of the horizontal pressure gradient. Now that term is incorporated into `∂xᶠᶜᶜ`.

As an aside, I think it is more efficient to evaluate dpdz directly, rather than recomputing buoyancy (which can be expensive, eg involving a 55 term formula).

I'm still not happy with how generalized coordinates are implemented, but I think additional changes should await a future PR and this is a good start.
title: Implement horizontal derivatives at constant z for `MutableVerticalDiscretization`

body:
## Summary
- Adds a new "Reduced-dimension fields with `indices`" section to the Field tutorial (`docs/src/fields.md`)
- Shows how to construct fields at a single index (e.g. surface slice) using the `indices` keyword
- Demonstrates `set!` on reduced fields and using `indices` with field operations (`Field(∂x(v) - ∂y(u), indices=...)`)
- All examples use `jldoctest` blocks with verified outputs

Closes #5206

## Test plan
- [x] Verify doctests pass: `julia --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.test("Oceananigans", test_args=["doctest"])'` or equivalent
- [x] Build docs locally and check the new section renders correctly

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Add fields with non-default indices to Field tutorial
